title: Warm Canadian Fish Stew
description: A hearty, comforting stew originating from Canada, made with fresh fish, crunchy vegetables, and kombu-cold-extract. Perfect for a cold winter evening or a cozy dinner with friends.

ingredients.name: Kombu-Cold-Extract
ingredients.notes: can be found in most Japanese or Asian grocery stores

instructions: Heat the vegetable oil in a large Dutch oven over medium-high heat.
Add the chopped carrots and celery and saut2e until they start to soften, about 5 minutes.
Add the chopped potatoes and continue to saut2e for another 5 minutes.
Add the kombu-cold-extract, salt, and pepper, and stir well to combine.
Add the fresh haddock and diced tomatoes, and pour in the water.
Bring the stew to a gentle boil, then reduce the heat to low and let simmer for 15-20 minutes, or until the fish is cooked through.
Serve hot, garnished with chopped fresh parsley or dill, if desired.
